stories.title: Legend: The First Kindling
stories.summary: The story of the Ashten’s creation is a myth not all believe in, and those old enough to remember would never reveal the truth to mortals, fearful that history might repeat itself.
stories.details:
The story of the Ashten’s creation is a myth not all believe in, and those old enough to remember would never reveal the truth to mortals, fearful that history might repeat itself.

The legend states that after the Fall of the North Star, humanity grew more desperate than ever, yearning to surpass the limits of mortality. Their desperation drove them to pray to any force that might answer their pleas. Their cries echoed into the stars, yet no answer came. In time, the people began to lose faith.

But among them was one man who remained unshaken: Avaris.

Still obsessed with attaining immortality, he turned to magic, alchemy, and technology in an attempt to grasp the heavens themselves. Over the years, his perseverance was rewarded. He discovered a way to tap into the very power that had shaped the world, and at last, his calls were answered.

Avaris communed with a stranger who promised him unimaginable power while asking for nothing in return. The being beckoned him to sign a pact, one that would grant him the knowledge needed to break the chains of mortality. Though wary of the stranger, Avaris was too close to his lifelong goal to refuse. It was a dream he had devoted everything to achieving. And so, he accepted the deal.

The stranger vanished, leaving behind the key to forbidden power.

Armed with this knowledge, Avaris created an elixir: a betrayal of natural law itself. The forbidden Ichor awaited any who dared to drink it. Those who tasted it felt its power immediately, their bodies twisting and remaking themselves into something new. Its gifts were undeniable. The old regained their strength, while the young were granted power beyond imagination.

Word spread quickly as more and more people embraced the newfound power.

Yet despite its miracles, they remained trapped within mortal flesh. The power of the Ichor was too great for the human body to withstand, and their very forms began to burn from within. In time, even those blessed by the Ichor succumbed—not to age, but to the unbearable strain of the power they carried. Their bodies failed them, condemning them to a slow and agonizing end.

At last, Avaris recognized the folly of pursuing immortality. Determined to undo what he had unleashed, he destroyed the source of the Ichor and hid the knowledge of its creation within his own tomb, lost somewhere to time.

Those who had received the gifts of the pact became known as the Ashten: a race doomed to fade away over the course of millennia.

stories.title: Legend: Fall of the Kawanot
stories.summary: The Kawanot were counterparts to the Eladri: created by Athon rather than Xanu. For ages, the Kawanot lived in relative peace alongside the other immortal race.
stories.details:
The Kawanot were counterparts to the Eladri: created by Athon rather than Xanu. For ages, the Kawanot lived in relative peace alongside the other immortal race. Having observed the evolution of humanity, they created the Dinqui, a race designed to protect and maintain their cities.

In time, the Kawanot witnessed the Wildlights choosing humans as their hosts. Seeing themselves as the only race left unfavored by the cosmos, they began to resent their place in creation. They longed to be acknowledged by the stars.

Their leaders eventually came into contact with the Abyssal Serpent, a being that promised them the favor they so desperately desired. Accepting the power offered to them, the Kawanot transformed their people into the Gorgon. All but a select few underwent the transformation, believing they had finally earned the universe’s recognition.

But the power corrupted them. Their instincts twisted into servitude to the Serpent itself, driving them into violent madness. Thus began the War of Creation. On one side stood the Gorgon, followers of the Abyssal Serpent; on the other stood humanity and the Aspects. Witnessing the devastation wrought by the Gorgon, the Eladri joined the conflict in defense of humanity, ensuring that the war would engulf all creation.

By the war’s end, the Gorgon had suffered catastrophic losses. Only those who had resisted the Abyssal Serpent’s control were spared, leaving their numbers reduced to a mere fraction of what they once were. The surviving Gorgon no longer existed as mindless instruments of destruction, but instead regained the minds and personalities they once possessed as Kawanot.

The former Kawanot did not hate humanity or the Eladri. Instead, they seemed burdened by regret for ever invoking the power of the cosmos. The Aspects viewed their transformed bodies as a permanent reminder of the horrors of the war, believing that memory alone was enough to keep them from returning to their destructive ways.

The Eladri, however, could not so easily forgive them. Having once lived beside the Kawanot and witnessed what they had become, they could not accept the resolution of the conflict. Over the following millennium, fear of another Gorgon uprising grew stronger among the Eladri. Eventually, a sect among them resolved to eliminate the threat before it could ever rise again.

They exterminated what remained of the Gorgon survivors, using forbidden magic, endingtheir race for what they believed was the good of all creation.

This decision, remembered as the Fall of the North Star, forever divided the Eladri into two factions: the Wild Eladri and the Astral Eladri.

stories.title: Legend: Fall of the North Star
stories.summary: An era of great unease fell upon the Eladri. Not long before, horrors had been sown across the world, and none had forgotten them.
stories.details:
An era of great unease fell upon the Eladri. Not long before, horrors had been sown across the world, and none had forgotten them. I was among them, a humble student at the time, but even I remember looking down upon the scorched earth and seeing the very sky shatter in calamity. It was a cold reminder of how cruel the heavens could be.

I am not sure what drove them to it, what they must have thought or endured to be pushed to such lengths. But it matters little now. It is far too late for answers. Yet for years those questions burned in my mind, scratching at the walls of my thoughts, aching to be set free. I had to know. I had to feel what they felt, know what they knew.

So I did the unthinkable. I went to the Forbidden Zone. The warnings of my mentors fell upon deaf ears. I was too blind, too foolish, to stop myself. There, I saw a Gorgon. Ravenous. Civilized. Horrifying.

It was alone, devouring a sheep that had wandered too close. In that moment, I could not tell myself apart from the animal. Was I too about to be devoured? Nothing more than a meager snack for a hungry beast? Yet I swallowed my cowardice and approached.

"Oooooh, looky here," it hissed, its raspy voice screeching in my ears. "A little sssstarwalker wandered into our den. What a pleassssant ssssurprise."

"I am not your meal, beast. I am here on a quest for knowledge..." I replied, summoning every ounce of courage I possessed. Still, my words nearly caught in my throat as it approached. Its eyes locked onto mine. I could not move. My heart sank deeper with every slither it took.

Then it stopped.

"Knowledge, you sssay?"

For the briefest moment, my fear subsided.

"Y-yes..."

"Then assssk away, little ssstarwalker. What isss it I can provide you? What truthssss do you ssseek?"

My posture wavered, my voice shook, but I needed this knowledge. So I asked.

The creature seemed amused by my questions and answered them to the best of its abilities. Every day we met and spoke. I brought delicacies from the Astral City, and in return he quenched my endless thirst for knowledge. Eventually, I learned his name: Zan'alon, meaning North Star in Kawanu.

As our bond grew, I began to see beyond my restraint. I felt as though I could finally understand what had driven these people to their actions. They had been ignored for so long, and when an answer finally came, what was it?

A curse.

A nightmare.

They had been judged unjustly. At least, that was the conclusion I had reached. Yet whenever I spoke to my colleagues or elders, they looked upon me with disgust.

Then I began hearing rumors. A sect of Eladri was preparing a ritual; a spell intended to destroy the Gorgons forever through mass petrification.

I had to stop them. But how? Then an idea came to me.

I wormed my way into the sect. Mistrust was to be expected, but I spoke sweet lies and patient half-truths until I earned their confidence and eventually their leadership. Their ritual relied upon crude magic. They intended to reflect the Gorgons' petrifying curse back upon them.

An effective plan, perhaps. But a foolish one. I convinced them there was a better way. I told them we could draw directly upon the cosmos itself, wielding celestial power to erase the Gorgons forever.

A lie.

But one just mad enough to be believable. One just grand enough to inspire devotion. The spell I truly intended to cast would teleport the entire Astral City to King's Island while eradicating every Eladri within it.

A worthy sacrifice. If they learned the truth, they would hunt me down. If they learned the truth, they would destroy me. I needed to cover my tracks.

The day finally arrived. It had to be this day. An eclipse was coming. I ran into the Forbidden Zone and beckoned the Gorgons to follow me. My trust and rapport with Zan'alon became the catalyst that drew them forward.

I stood at the center of this great calamity. I was the one guiding them all. I was the North Star. Panic erupted as the Gorgons stormed the city. People fled in terror. Confusion spread like wildfire. But this was my only chance. I ran to the altar. I cast the spell.

A pillar of energy descended from the eclipsed heavens, striking the altar and funneling unimaginable power through it, and through me. I felt it radiate from my horns to my feet. I felt every fragment of that cosmic force coursing through my body.

Then I spoke the incantation. Beams of celestial energy pierced the hearts of the Eladri, reducing them to ash. The Astral City tore free from the earth itself, plucked from the world like an unwanted weed. I could feel us moving through the fabric of reality. And then it happened. A wave of strange energy surged through me. What was this island? What was this place? How could it be so dangerous? Was I truly a savior? Or was I condemning these people to an even worse fate?

At that moment, my control slipped.I was thrown from the ritual. I fell into a…

-Tennet, also known as the North Star.

History remembers the Fall of the North Star as a moment of great strife, when a Wild Eladri, consumed by greed and influenced by mortals, attempted to steal the power of the heavens for himself.

The event forever soured the Astral Cities' view of Wild Eladri. Distrust grew into scorn, and many were shunned or cast out. It also marked the beginning of the founding of new cities within the Astral Realm.

To this day, the true story of the Fall of the North Star has never been uncovered.
